segments.text: But now, O Job, hear my speech, and listen to all my words.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Behold, I will open my mouth; my address is on the tip of my tongue.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: My words are from an upright heart, and my lips speak sincerely what I know.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Refute me if you can; prepare your case and confront me.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I am just like you before God; I was also formed from clay.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Surely no fear of me should terrify you; nor will my hand be heavy upon you.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Surely you have spoken in my hearing, and I have heard these very words:
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: ‘I am pure, without transgression; I am clean, with no iniquity in me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yet God finds occasions against me; He counts me as His enemy.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He puts my feet in the stocks; He watches over all my paths.’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Behold, you are not right in this matter. I will answer you, for God is greater than man.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Why do you complain to Himthat He answers nothing a man asks?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: For God speaks in one way and in another, yet no one notices.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: In a dream, in a vision in the night, when deep sleep falls upon menas they slumber on their beds,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He opens their ears and terrifies them with warnings
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: to turn a man from wrongdoing and keep him from pride,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: to preserve his soul from the Pit and his life from perishing by the sword.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: A man is also chastened on his bedwith pain and constant distress in his bones,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: so that he detests his bread, and his soul loathes his favorite food.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: His flesh wastes away from sight, and his hidden bones protrude.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He draws near to the Pit, and his life to the messengers of death.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Yet if there is a messenger on his side, one mediator in a thousand, to tell a man what is right for him,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: to be gracious to him and say,‘Spare him from going down to the Pit; I have found his ransom,’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: then his flesh is refreshed like a child’s; he returns to the days of his youth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He prays to God and finds favor; he sees God’s face and shouts for joy, and God restores His righteousnessto that man.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then he sings before menwith these words:‘I have sinned and perverted what was right; yet I did not get what I deserved.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He redeemed my soul from going down to the Pit, and I will live to see the light.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Behold, all these things God does to a man, two or even three times,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: to bring back his soul from the Pit, that he may be enlightened with the light of life.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Pay attention, Job, and listen to me; be silent, and I will speak.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: But if you have something to say, answer me; speak up, for I would like to vindicate you.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: But if not, then listen to me; be quiet, and I will teach you wisdom.
segments.speaker: Elihu
